name: js-10print
title: Guided Intro to JS
description: In this intro lesson, you'll learn how to bring the classic 10PRINT algorithm to life in JavaScript while exploring core programming concepts like variables, loops, conditionals, functions, and randomness. You'll also learn how to make your code interactive using event listeners and how to simplify your work using libraries.

inline-js.title: adding JavaScript to an HTML page

arithmetic.template:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/styles.css">
<body></body>
<script>

	const a = 9000
	const b = 3.14
	{{code}}

</script>

const-vs-let.template:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/styles.css">
<body></body>
<script>

	const a = 9000
	const b = 3.14
	const c = 10 + a / 3

	{{code}}

</script>

reassignment.template:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/styles.css">
<body></body>
<script>

	const a = 9000
	const b = 3.14
	const c = 10 + a / 3

	let x = 100
	x = 200

</script>

booleans.title: booleans

boolean-expr.template:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/styles.css">
<body></body>
<script>

	let loggedIn = false
	
	// later we can change it
	loggedIn = true
	
	{{code}}

</script>
boolean-expr.code:
const a = 100 > 50 // true
	const b = 10 < 5   // false
	const c = 20 >= 20 // true
	const d = 40 === 2 // false

string-plus.template:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/styles.css">
<body></body>
<script>

	let greet = 'hello there!'
	greet = "hello there!"
	greet = `hello there!`
	
	{{code}}

</script>

number-plus-string.template:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/styles.css">
<body></body>
<script>

	let greet = 'hello there!'
	greet = "hello there!"
	greet = `hello there!`
	
	const user = 'Nick'
	greet = 'hello there ' + user
	
	{{code}}

</script>
number-plus-string.code:
let i = 100
	let message = 'My favorite number is ' + i

calling-functions.title: calling functions
calling-functions.template:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/styles.css">
<body></body>
<script>

	let greet = 'hello there!'
	greet = "hello there!"
	greet = `hello there!`
	
	const user = 'Nick'
	greet = 'hello there ' + user
	
	let i = 100
	let message = 'My favorite number is ' + i
	
	{{code}}

</script>

pass-args.title: functions: passing arguments
pass-args.template:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/styles.css">
<body></body>
<script>

	let greet = 'hello there!'
	greet = "hello there!"
	greet = `hello there!`
	
	const user = 'Nick'
	greet = 'hello there ' + user
	
	let i = 100
	let message = 'My favorite number is ' + i
	
	{{code}}

</script>

conditional-repeat.template:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/styles.css">
<body></body>
<script>

	if (Math.random() < 0.5) {
		document.body.innerText += '／'
	} else {
		document.body.innerText += '＼'

nn-on.template:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/styles.css">
<body></body>
<script src="js/nn.min.js"></script>
<script>
	/* global nn */

	function changeBackground () {
		const r = Math.random() * 255
		const g = Math.random() * 255
		const b = Math.random() * 255
		const bg = `rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})`
		document.body.style.background = bg
	}
	
	function tenPrint () {
		document.body.innerText = ''
		for (let i = 0; i < 600; i++) {
			if (Math.random() < 0.5) {
				document.body.innerText += '／'
			} else {
				document.body.innerText += '＼'
			}
		} 
	}
	
	nn.on('load', tenPrint)
	nn.on('click', tenPrint)
	nn.on('click', changeBackground)

</script>

	/* global nn */

				document.body.innerText += '＼'
			}
		} 
	}
	
	nn.on('load', tenPrint)
	nn.on('click', tenPrint)
	nn.on('click', changeBackground)

</script>

nn-css.template:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/styles.css">
<body></body>
<script src="js/nn.min.js"></script>
<script>
	/* global nn */

	function changeBackground () {
		const hue = nn.random(360)
		const clr = nn.hsl(hue, 100, 20)
		nn.get('body').{{code}}
	}
	
	function tenPrint () {
		document.body.innerText = ''
		for (let i = 0; i < 600; i++) {
			if (Math.random() < 0.5) {
				document.body.innerText += '／'
			} else {
				document.body.innerText += '＼'
			}
		} 
	}
	
	nn.on('load', tenPrint)
	nn.on('click', tenPrint)
	nn.on('click', changeBackground)

</script>
